fragility_points: Short-horizon BTC volatility before Apr. 17 noon ET.
Binance-specific print risk because the market is venue-specific.
key_assumptions: The current Binance BTC/USDT above-70k regime persists through Apr. 17 noon ET.
No major shock or Binance-specific dislocation pushes the one-minute close below 70,000.
main_logical_chain: Check governing contract mechanics and market-implied baseline.
Use same-venue Binance BTC/USDT history to judge whether the threshold is currently in or out of the money.
Apply an outside-view discount because exact-minute threshold markets are more fragile than daily-close intuition suggests.
Conclude Yes is highly likely but not as close to certainty as the market implies.
main_thesis: BTC is already trading comfortably above 70k on Binance, so Yes is the clear base-rate favorite for Apr. 17 noon ET, but the market slightly underprices exact-minute volatility risk.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone ... has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified in the title.
Apr. 7 through Apr. 14 daily Binance closes all remained above 70,000.
source_quality_view: Primary evidence quality is high because Binance is the same venue/pair as settlement, but the direct price-history check used daily candles rather than the eventual exact one-minute settlement candle; Polymarket rules provide necessary contract interpretation.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract settles on one exact Binance one-minute close, so a brief sharp selloff can still produce No.
BTC can move several percent within a few days, making sub-70k by settlement unlikely but not rare enough to ignore.
strongest_supports: Binance daily data shows eight straight daily closes above 70k from Apr. 7 through Apr. 14.
Recent closes and lows stayed above the threshold, indicating some cushion rather than a knife-edge setup.
Polymarket ladder shape is broadly consistent with spot trading in the mid-70k area.
timing_relevance: Resolution depends on the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close on Apr. 17, 2026; 12:00 ET maps to 16:00 UTC during EDT.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact operational representation of the noon ET candle on Binance UI/API should still be checked near settlement.
Any new macro or crypto shock before settlement could quickly compress the threshold cushion.
what_would_change_view: I would cut the estimate if BTC trades back toward 71k or below before settlement, if repeated threshold tests appear, or if Binance-specific anomalies emerge.
